topic: Someone finds a wallet containing a large sum of cash
raw_output:
[story 1]

The worn leather of the wallet felt greasy under my fingertips. I picked it up from the damp pavement, a crumpled heap of cheap material and paper. Inside, a thick wad of bills spilled out, a startling amount of money. A slow, deliberate movement traced the edges of the paper with my thumb, a faint, dismissive sigh escaping my lips. This was just another piece of trash, carelessly discarded. I didn't feel any rush of excitement, only a dull, heavy sense of being inconvenienced by something utterly worthless. I tucked it into my pocket, the weight a meaningless anchor against my thigh.

[story 2]

My gaze swept over the contents of the abandoned purse. The sheer volume of currency was staggering, an absurd display of careless wealth. I let my eyes linger for a moment too long, a slight tightening around my jaw. It was such an obvious display, this kind of fortune left lying around. I shook my head, a small, almost imperceptible movement. "Honestly," I muttered to the empty air, my voice flat and edged with impatience, "some people have no sense of discretion." I didn't plan on taking it; it was simply an unsightly mess that needed to be dealt with, like any other piece of refuse.

[story 3]

The crisp banknotes felt alien against my palm. I stood there for a moment, observing the scene—the discarded wallet, the glittering cascade of cash. A deep furrow settled between my brows. It was so brazen, this kind of display. I didn't bother examining the identification inside; the purpose of the money was irrelevant to me. With a sharp flick of my wrist, I gathered the money, shoving it quickly into my coat pocket. A faint smirk played on my lips, a fleeting expression of profound superiority, as if I were merely tidying up someone else's mess.
